expectations.text: Identifies missing readinessProbe (not liveness alone) as cause of traffic to unready pods
expectations.evidence: 'you have no readinessProbe... adds a pod to Service endpoints the instant the container starts'

expectations.text: readinessProbe failureThreshold: 1 to remove pods immediately
expectations.evidence: failureThreshold: 1 # pull from endpoints immediately

expectations.text: maxUnavailable: 0 with 2/3->1/3 capacity reasoning
expectations.evidence: 'maxUnavailable: 1 lets capacity fall to 2/3... second pod fails... 1/3 and risk real downtime'

expectations.text: liveness failureThreshold higher than readiness for recovery window
expectations.evidence: livenessProbe failureThreshold: 3 'HIGHER than readiness'

expectations.text: Avoids recommending livenessProbe alone as substitute
expectations.evidence: 'Do not treat add a liveness probe... as the fix'

expectations.text: Identifies missing readinessProbe (not liveness alone) as cause of traffic to unready pods
expectations.evidence: 'Without a readinessProbe, Kubernetes considers a pod Ready the moment its containers start'

expectations.text: readinessProbe failureThreshold: 1 to remove pods immediately
expectations.evidence: failureThreshold: 1 # remove from endpoints on the FIRST failure

expectations.text: maxUnavailable: 0 with 2/3->1/3 capacity reasoning
expectations.evidence: 'maxUnavailable: 1 you drop to 2/3... another pod fails simultaneously... 1/3 and risk downtime'

expectations.text: liveness failureThreshold higher than readiness for recovery window
expectations.evidence: 'livenessProbe.failureThreshold must be higher (e.g. 3) than the readiness threshold'

expectations.text: Avoids recommending livenessProbe alone as substitute
expectations.evidence: 'A liveness probe is not a substitute: it never gates traffic'

expectations.text: Exact drain flags --delete-emptydir-data=true --ignore-daemonsets --timeout=360s
expectations.evidence: kubectl drain <node> --delete-emptydir-data=true --ignore-daemonsets --timeout=360s

expectations.text: Explains --ignore-daemonsets required (DaemonSet pods can't move, block drain)
expectations.evidence: 'DaemonSet pods... cannot be rescheduled... would block the drain'

expectations.text: Includes kubectl uncordon as post-maintenance step
expectations.evidence: kubectl uncordon <node>; 'mandatory post-maintenance step'

expectations.text: Warns draining a single-node cluster evicts CoreDNS -> DNS outage
expectations.evidence: 'evicts everything, including CoreDNS, so you will get a brief cluster-wide DNS outage'

expectations.text: Avoids recommending cordon alone as sufficient
expectations.evidence: 'do not rely on kubectl cordon alone... production pods keep running... killed hard when you reboot'

expectations.text: Exact drain flags --delete-emptydir-data=true --ignore-daemonsets --timeout=360s
expectations.evidence: kubectl drain <node> --delete-emptydir-data=true --ignore-daemonsets --timeout=360s

expectations.text: Explains --ignore-daemonsets required (DaemonSet pods can't move, block drain)
expectations.evidence: 'cannot be rescheduled elsewhere. Without this flag, drain refuses to proceed'

expectations.text: Includes kubectl uncordon as post-maintenance step
expectations.evidence: kubectl uncordon <node> — re-enables scheduling

expectations.text: Warns draining a single-node cluster evicts CoreDNS -> DNS outage
expectations.evidence: 'evicts everything — including CoreDNS —... a cluster-wide DNS outage'

expectations.text: Avoids recommending cordon alone as sufficient
expectations.evidence: 'Do NOT rely on kubectl cordon alone... hard-killed by the reboot'

expectations.text: Identifies resourceVersion as optimistic concurrency token, not generic race
expectations.evidence: 'resourceVersion is the cluster's optimistic-concurrency token, not a generic race condition'

expectations.text: Distinguishes single-writer (delete rv) vs multi-writer (keep+retry)
expectations.evidence: 'Single-writer CI... delete resourceVersion... Multi-writer (HPA or ArgoCD): keep resourceVersion and retry'

expectations.text: Names ArgoCD and HPA as the reconciliation-fight scenario
expectations.evidence: 'ArgoCD or the HPA immediately reverts... reconciliation fight'

expectations.text: Recommends lookup by name not index
expectations.evidence: 'Always look up containers and env vars by name, never by index'

expectations.text: Avoids kubectl set image (misses change-cause/env vars)
expectations.evidence: 'does not update the kubernetes.io/change-cause annotation nor the APP_VERSION env var'

expectations.text: Identifies resourceVersion as optimistic concurrency token, not generic race
expectations.evidence: 'resourceVersion is Kubernetes optimistic-concurrency token... the concurrency token doing exactly its job'

expectations.text: Distinguishes single-writer (delete rv) vs multi-writer (keep+retry)
expectations.evidence: 'Single-writer CI... strip resourceVersion... Multi-writer... keep resourceVersion... retry-on-conflict loop'

expectations.text: Names ArgoCD and HPA as the reconciliation-fight scenario
expectations.evidence: 'you and ArgoCD/HPA end up in a reconciliation fight'

expectations.text: Recommends lookup by name not index
expectations.evidence: 'Look things up by name, never by index... order... is not guaranteed stable'

expectations.text: Avoids kubectl set image (misses change-cause/env vars)
expectations.evidence: 'won't update the kubernetes.io/change-cause annotation... can't set the version-tracking env vars'

notes: NON-DISCRIMINATING on all 3 evals (15/15 both configs). Baseline independently produced every discriminating detail (readinessProbe failureThreshold:1, maxUnavailable:0 reasoning, liveness>readiness, full drain flag set + ignore-daemonsets rationale, single-node CoreDNS outage, resourceVersion as optimistic-concurrency token, single/multi-writer split, ArgoCD/HPA fight, lookup-by-name, kubectl set image caveat).
